title: Number of people with extensively drug resistant tuberculosis
subtitle: Estimated number of people with [extensively drug resistant tuberculosis](#dod:drug-resistant-tuberculosis).
note: Data includes tuberculosis cases in [HIV](#dod:hiv)-negative people. Tuberculosis cases among people with HIV are classified under [HIV/AIDS](#dod:hiv_aids) cases.
caption:
Number of people with extensively drug resistant tuberculosis

Estimated number of people with extensively drug resistant tuberculosis.

This choropleth world map displays the estimated number of people with extensively drug resistant tuberculosis in 2023. The legend uses a color scale ranging from light beige for zero cases to dark red for 10,000 cases, with intermediate thresholds at 10, 30, 100, 300, 1,000, and 3,000. The darkest red band, representing values between 3,000 and 10,000, is concentrated in a few specific countries: India, China, Russia, and the Philippines clearly fall into this highest category. Surrounding these high-burden nations, countries such as Indonesia, Pakistan, and Ukraine appear in the next darkest band, indicating case numbers between 1,000 and 3,000. A broad cluster of nations across Eastern Europe, Central Asia, and parts of South America, including Brazil and Peru, sit in the mid-range orange shades corresponding to 100 to 1,000 cases. Much of Western Europe, North America, and Australia are depicted in the lightest shades, signifying fewer than 10 or 30 cases. Greenland and several small island nations are marked with a hatched pattern indicating no data. The data presented includes tuberculosis cases in HIV-negative people, while tuberculosis cases among people with HIV are classified under HIV/AIDS cases. The visual distribution highlights a significant concentration of cases in specific regions of Asia and Eastern Europe compared to the lower counts observed in the Americas and Western Europe.
